review_text:
[Highly recommend] I recently visited Mentabkhetna restaurant, and it was truly a great experience. From the warm and friendly greeting at the door to every dish that we ordered.
First, the atmosphere was cozy and the seating arrangements were comfortable.
We started things off with a few cold mezzas. Hummus was so creamy, baba ghanouj was flavorful, and the tabbouleh was refreshing.
For the main course, it was served hot and fresh, and the portions were generous.
Overall, I can't recommend Mentabkhetna enough. If you're looking for authentic and innovative Lebanese cuisine in a welcoming and intimate setting, this is the spot for you. I can't wait to return and try more of their delicious dishes.

review_text:
[Disappointing Dining Experience at Chez Maguy] Our visit to Chez Maguy in Batroun was a letdown from start to finish. The tabbouleh was overwhelmingly salty, the batata harra and fried fish were undercooked, and the grilled shrimp hadn’t been cleaned, leaving us to do it ourselves at the table. On top of that, the service was frustrating—an older gentleman from the staff took our salad plate before we were done and insisted it was finished, despite our objection.

We also ordered a grilled fish based on their recommendation, and while the choice itself was good, we were shocked when the bill arrived—$40 for a single piece of fish. Between the poorly prepared food, the dismissive service, and the unexpected price tag, this was not an experience I’d want to repeat or recommend.

review_text:
[Fraud and Very bad experience] I had one of the worst dining experiences ever at Swiss Butter in Batroun. The food was mediocre at best, but the real problem was the dishonest way they handle orders. The waitress recommended a ribeye that wasn’t even on the menu and never told us the price — which turned out to be a shocking $55 per plate. We ordered it twice, still never being informed of the cost, only to find out at the end when the bill came. This is deceptive, unethical, and unacceptable. Any restaurant with integrity tells customers the price before selling something, especially when it’s outrageously overpriced. The service was disappointing, the experience left a bad taste in every way, and I will warn others so they don’t get ripped off the way we did.

review_text:
["Exorbitant Prices and Slow Service Mar Otherwise Promising Venue"] The venue boasts a commendable location, strategically positioned near the highway in northern Lebanon, not far from the city of Batroun. They offer an electric cart service to transport guests from the entrance to the beach, where an array of comfortable tables and chairs are available. The service, albeit courteous, is noticeably slow. The DJ requires a considerable amount of time to play invigorating music; initially, the music was exceedingly monotonous, only improving after 10:00 PM when livelier tunes finally emerged. Unfortunately, by this time, a significant portion of my friends had already left, disheartened by the earlier dull atmosphere. 

The food, while decent, is exorbitantly priced. A bottle of gin, which typically costs $25, was outrageously marked up to $128. Our modest consumption of some snacks and soft drinks, along with the gin, culminated in a staggering bill of $462. The pricing is egregiously inflated, posing a severe detriment to tourism. In numerous other countries with far superior infrastructure (such as roads, cleanliness, and service) — like Spain, France, Italy, Greece, Kazakhstan, and even Dubai — the maximum expenditure per person, including excellent food and beverages, would be approximately $40. Adding insult to injury, the valet parking demanded an additional $5.50 as a fee. In many countries, parking is complimentary when dining at a restaurant.

In Lebanon, it seems exploitation is rampant, extending beyond just the banks and political system. I intend to boycott all restaurants and pubs this summer and strongly urge others to do the same to compel these establishments to reevaluate their prices and cease their exploitative practices.

review_text:
[Rude Service and Poor Quality at Oysters – Never Again] I had a very disappointing experience at Oysters this year. We ordered grilled calamari and were served only the tentacles instead of the actual calamari, which absolutely did not match the price. When we complained, both the waiter and the manager were extremely rude, insisting “this is how we serve it.” We even showed them the restaurant’s own Instagram photos, which clearly showed a completely different dish, but the manager dismissed us by saying those pictures are just for advertising.

I visited Oysters back in 2023 and had a wonderful experience — but this time, it was the complete opposite. The fish were tasteless, the fried shrimps were served with the heads and not cleaned properly, and the calamari was a total disaster. Not only did they refuse to apologize, but the manager was unprofessional, impolite, and even threw some unnecessary words at us before walking away.

We didn’t mind paying for the dish, but we simply didn’t want it. Instead of handling the situation with professionalism, the management’s attitude made everything worse. Honestly, this visit wasn’t even worth one star.

[Nice restaurant by the seaside] My friend and I stumbled on this restaurant by accident. We had gone to see the Phoenician wall, and we parked right in front of the restaurant just by the sea.
The staff were very nice, especially a young lady, I forgot to ask her name. They took their time to explain the dishes to us. We had Fattouch, tabbouleh, and grilled pig fish. The food was very nice, as was the lemonade.
Oysters are their specialty, so I decided to have one (my first time ever). I am sure I will try it again.

review_text:
[IF IN BATROUN AND YOU SEE A RESTAURANT CALLED OYSTERS THST LOOKS SO INVITING FOR A SUNSET, DO NOT GO THERE!] My last night in Lebanon, I go to watch the sunset and see this charming seafood place called Oysters near St Estephan Church.
I ask for oysters, the one server who spoke English said they didn’t have any, then said he would check. 10 minutes later he says yes, they have 6. I’ve eaten oysters all over the world and this place looked safe…..but it is NOT.
The oysters looked funny and had a white substance dripping off them. The server told me it was “the sauce they put on them”. Sauce? What? So I tried one with the least milky stuff on it and it tasted rancid. I spit it out into a napkin. 
I told the staff they are bad, and these were likely sitting out for hours.
They insisted I had to pay, and get this, they were 5 USDs an oyster!!! No oysters in the US are that expensive.
I was told to wait for the owner. 30 minutes later he walks up and I explained what happened. The 2 servers who served me ran off in their car. Apparently another oyster was missing. I swore I did not even eat the one and spit it out. 
He told me there was nothing he could do, he must pay for each oyster. I told him if he went to a restaurant and the food was bad, would he pay? He said “Of course”
WRONG!!!!
AVOID THIS PLACE! Maybe I was naive and stupid to try and eat oysters in Lebanon, but I have eaten many places and should’ve really been more careful, but just as a warning to others….DO NOT EAT HERE!
I usually write good reviews, but this could be one of my worst ones ever!

review_text:
[BEST SEAFOOD IN BATROUN!] This seafood restaurant stood out as a culinary revelation that defied our expectations. Despite initially being overshadowed by a couple of other higher-ranked seafood restaurants in the area, we decided to give Bistr’eau a try as our first meal, upon arriving in Batroun. Little did we know, it would turn out to be the culinary highlight of our stay, ultimately the place we selected as our last meal and the grand finale of our time in Batroun.

Situated beneath a series of picturesque succession of ornate arches along the waterfront, within one of Batroun's historical buildings at the port of the Batroûn Old Souk, this restaurant stood out as a true outlier. What made this dining experience truly exceptional was the symphony of flavors orchestrated by the fusion of cuisines, the inventive pairing of traditional and rarely-used ingredients, and the meticulous execution of each dish. Between our two visits, we embarked on a culinary journey through almost the entire menu, relishing every morsel.

The appetizers alone were a testament to the culinary prowess at play – from the refreshing Fattouch to the innovative Hummus with shrimp, the smoky allure of the aubergine with sundried tomato tarator, and fresh zaatar. The culinary exploration continued with Grilled Calamari, Sicilian octopus, shrimp fokhara with tomato sauce, and perfectly roasted Patatas Bravas. All this food only served to pave the way for the triumphant main courses—seafood risotto and the pièce de résistance, a perfectly seasoned deep-fried whole fish. The culinary symphony reached its crescendo with the Pain Perdu à la mode, offering the perfect sweet note to conclude our culinary odyssey.

review_text:
[At Ibn Battuta mall] Located in Ibn Battuta mall, we visited this beautiful restaurant for late lunch. The red and white theme of the restaurant interior gives it a bright and vibrant look. Felt a very positive vibes the moment we entered the restaurant.

We started with the Functional Juices and both were very refreshing & Heavenly in taste. Start your meals with these, highly recomended. Rise and shine which is a blend of Oats, Honey, Yougurt, Berries & Bananna. Made well without excess sweet and healthy. Kale & Pineapple with is Kale, Mango, Spinach & Pineapple. This has good health benefits for those who are looking for healthy options.

Mushroom Soup was very nice consistency and very thick. We loved the taste of the spices mixed with it. The Burratta salad is no doubt another best seller. Freshly made Burratta cheese which was very soft and tasty.

Grilled shrimp skewers on a bed of Sauteed veggies is another fantastic dish. The shrimps do not smell cause the marination is perfect and we get the taste of the mild marination spices mixed with shrimps. The Turkey Club Sandwitch is another killer. Taste of the Eggs with Veggies inside is superb. Grilled salmon with Quinoa was very well done and the Salmon did not smell strong but instead we can feel the taste in our mouths. Usually a strong smell of the Salmon is a out off, so the Chef has handled this very nicely. A must have for Salmon lovers.

The Apple and Celery drink is another superb drink. All the shakes and Smoothies are made by Mary Jane a trained expert in juices.

The Strawberry Pistachio Cheesecake was the one we selected from their display of mouth watering Desserts. The tangy sauce with the sweetness underneath was a riot of taste in our taste buds. Later we had their Pistachio & Caramel Ice Creams. All these Desserts are a must have to satiate your cravings.

The restaurant supervisor Mary Jane deserve a special mention, her mannerisms and knowledge about the food were praiseworthy.

Overall we had a great experience here. Service is good. Taste, quality and presentation of the food is praiseworthy.

Donot mind paying but the waiter took advantage of us.

review_text:
[Interesting Concept-Burger About 80% There] Ryan our server did a good job with us.  The place is newish to the Mirdif area of Dubai and in the middle of the residential area.  They deliver.  The store has an American diner motif but Country Music in the background and serves European potato fries?  Not joking.

I had the Comanche Burger.  Big patty, cheddar cheese, fried onion ring, beef bacon, and their 'Black Forest' sauce that is supposed to be a mushroom and blue cheese sauce.  The burger was fresh made and served hot.  Everybody knows blue cheese makes everything 'pop'.  I couldn't taste the blue cheese at all.  This made the burger disappointing.  My partner had the Bo Burger, a standard, near homemade style America burger, and she enjoyed her fare.

All said, I might go back because everything was fresh and hot, but not likely to have the Comanche.

review_text:
[BIG NO TO THIS RESTAURANT] Went for dinner yesterday after reading good reviews in zomato but it wasn’t upto mark honestly *Totally failed our expectation* - I would Highly recommend to visit KABAB KARACHI & DELHI NIHARI instead of wasting money in spice hut. Reason 👇🏻👇🏻
Ordered chicken bihari rolls, butter chicken, delhi nehari, paratha, tandoori roti & naan. 
First of all, there is no seating area as this restaurant is very small in size. One of the staff suggested us that they will serve us at car and we agreed as we were hell hungry after tiring shopping. 
1. Chicken bihari roll wasn’t spicy at all & was average in taste, nothing wow at all plus it was so small & tiny in size for 10dhs, not worth it honestly - better to go KABAB KARACHI in Al nahda as its’ spicy, tastiest, filling and worth the money (9dhs). 
2. Delhi nihari dish was so average in taste and was more of salt in it. BIG NO and better to go DELHI NIHARI as its’ delicious and more in quantity.
3. Butter chicken wasn’t bad but still not worth it as its’ less in quantity for 27dhs - better to go SINDH SHAHBAZ RESTAURANT as it is perfect in taste and quantity.
4. Now coming to paratha, tandoori roti & naan - only naan was good as paratha & tandoori roti was average in taste & size - actually all three of them are small in size for 2dhs - 2.5dhs. 
I so wanted to eat karachi rabri but after disaster experience, quit to BASKIN ROBIN. 

I wish, I would have clicked photos of their poor presentation & just three small pieces of beef in nihari but I couldn’t, was so hungry that I forgot :p  

Never visiting this restaurant for sure........

review_text:
[Brunch in style right where you’re seated at!] Journey:
We were invited to try out thier new brunch at the Mirdiff branch. Since, brunches and fridays go hand in hand in Dubai so we could not wait to experience it for ourselves.

Ambiance:
The moment you make your way towards LOL, you have a coffee & tea counter setup, where you can have a brew of your choice and right next to them are thier delicious pastries lined up. There is a casual vibe and the dim lighting to enjoy your weekend. The seating is comfy which includes couches and chairs. It is perfect either way if you are part of a large group or planning a small affair, the cafe will suit your needs.

Rating: 7.5 Golden Nuggets

Bite Board

LOL launched a new concept for the brunch where the food comes to you instead of you walking around in circles deciding what to eat. Also, it is an unlimited brunch where you can keep refilling your plates with servings of the dish you like. The brunch is priced at AED 79 and is available on Fridays and Saturdays.

Drinks: There are options for strawberry and peach lemonade, fresh orange juice and water. We tried the orange juice and strawberry lemonade. Both the drinks were refreshing.

French Toast & Pancakes: A well-cooked french toast with a dash of cinnamon and topped with fresh berries served along with maple syrup. It was our favorite dish that we could not help ourselves from a second serving, YES THAT GOOD! The pancakes were fluffy and topped with raspberry sauce but were dry texture wise.

Choice of Eggs: There are two options where you could choose between a scrambled/boiled/fried egg or a mushroom omelette. We went for a scrambled egg that was served on top of a sourdough bread.

Breakfast Rosti: A platter with chili chickpeas, bacon, cherry tomatoes, mushrooms, roasted potatoes and sausages. Our friend was a huge fan of the chickpeas dish as it was something different and had an amazing flavor to it.

Red Quinoa Salad: The combination of beetroot and quinoa was superb. Usually, we are not huge fans of quinoa but this totally changed our minds and we would not mind a repeat of it.

Moutabal: We could not taste this as there were no breads along with it. Our suggestion would be to add some arabic bread or bread-sticks in the future.

Tunisian Chicken Meatballs: A bit different from the regular meatballs which usually involves red meat. These chicken meatballs had the right amount of spices and were cooked perfectly.

Prawn Penne Arrabbiata: Since our friend was a vegetarian, we requested for the pasta without the prawns. The pasta was cooked well but we would have preferred the tomatoes to be slightly less, guess its just how the dish was and does not suit our palate.

Roast Harissa Chicken: The dish would have been another one of our favorites if only the chicken was not too dry and too hard to cut through.

Desert Platter: It included carrot cake, red velvet cake and macarons. Both the cakes were soft and melted in your mouth but the macarons were a miss for us.

Service:
This is an aspect that needs to be worked on, the staff needed to be called multiple times to hear out our request.

Cash Outflow:
The price is quite reasonable for the unlimited brunch. 

Location:
26B Street, Mirdiff Park Center, Dubai

Circling Back:
We shall make another visit to try their desserts and coffee.

[Bad] 20% of their food is good, the rest is bad. No sashimi, the sushi does not look good at all, their juices are so bad, and let's better not even talk about the pizza.
But most of all, we were the only customers in the restaurant (and now we know why) but we were getting almost no attention from the staff, they were just chit-chatting or busy scrolling their phones. I really do not think this branch will survive for long in a competitive market like Dubai. I am sorry to say, but they do not deserve customers.

review_text:
[Disgusting Seating Area & Terrible Service, Rude Staff who talks about you in their native Tagalog language!!] Not sure what I can exactly pin point here in this review because there's nothung good about Sushi Nations (Uptown Mirdif Branch) . 
Let me start with:
1) The hygiene issue: It's None Existent! Seating Area is gross, stains all over the sofas, the tables are dirty, even glasses are stained and not properly washed, probably just rinsed off before it is being used to serve another customer and that's it!!
2) The food: I can honestly say it's not good at all. It taste awful. 40% of the sushi rolls were canned tuna!!! and another 40% were crab and only 20% were veggies and salmon rolls. 
Now, the soups, they smell and taste like garbage!!
And it will take you around 1.5 hours till you get the first appetizer mini bowl. 
Then you'll have to wait 2 more hours until the sushi rolls are ready!! 
I got food poisoning when I went to their restaurant, started vomiting and had severe diarrhea right afterwards.
3) The service: When you first come in, if you're lucky, there will be 1 staff in any of the corners that will acknowledge you.
Then you pick a table, sit down and just wait and wait for a staff member to come in and take your order.
And from what it seems to me and to the group of friends I was with, that the staff don't like to work there. They are so depressed, slowly moving around and so irritated.
They talk down to you and with a loud voice. The Filipino chef there was making fun of me and my friends, IN FRONT OF ME!!! Saying demeaning things about Arabs, even though they live and work in an Arabic country, in Middle East, without even any regards or respect to me and my friends as customer and without even considering that I, maybe, speak Tagalog. 
I have actually took many courses, specifically to understand the language of many UAE residents and to call them out when they mock us, in their mother tongue, in front of us, assuming we are so oblivious and have no idea what they are talking about!!!! #SHAME

They Manager then came on the phone and again said the same and as I was getting upset as we are leaving the house in 15mins he hung up the phone on me. Then he called back in 10mins and said do you want the rest of the order or what. 

I order from this place once a month and never faced this service from them. 

Very bad attitude and of course they have now lost a loyal client...

No food for the picnic thanks Eva Kana for not even trying to accommodate a loyal client

review_text:
[Delicious food @chin] Delicious food @chinchinuae (Mirdif branch)!

I ordered food through Zomato. Though Food was  supposed to be delivered in 1 hour, it took good  1 hr and 40minutes. But it was worth the wait. Food was piping hot, I think that’s why it got late as everything was freshly made.

My order was:

1.       Hot and sour soup

2.       Lunch set ( Crispy chilli chicken dry, sweet and sour fish, Chicken fried rice).

3.       Lunch set (Vegetable schezwan noodles, chicken hot garlic, stir fried Chinese greens, vegetable spring rolls).

4.       Lunch set (egg schezwan rice, chicken Manchurian, stir fried Chinese greens, veg spring rolls.

Large portion size! Fried rice and noodles were generously loaded with vegetables. Mildly spiced dishes. They give some extra sauces too. Some Chinese restaurants meals contain too much salt but here food was not extra salty. Great place for desi style Chinese cuisine!

 #little_pakistani_kitchen

review_text:
[Desi Chinese? has to be ChinChin] My Indian tastebuds were craving for some desi chinese, you know the kind we grab on monsoon nights, and what came to my mind was ChinChin. Quickly checked their IG page and was drooling, also saw their current discount promo and that was it.

We tried

Starters
1. Cottage Cheese Chilli Dry
2. Crispy Babycorn Hot Garlic Dry

Mains
1. Veg Manchurian
2. Mixed Veg in Schezwan Sauce

Rice
Vegetable Schezwan Fried Rice

Noodles
Vegetable Hakka Noodles

I would highly recommend Crispy Babycorn, Veg Manchurian with Schezwan Fried Rice and Hakka Noodles.

Packaging was acceptable according to covid-19 guidelines, they deliver all over Dubai and Sharjah, are very quick and true to their timings. They've got a forever (hopefully) customer in me.

review_text:
[This is the first place I] This is the first place I have seen that actually makes really good pizza and really good burgers at the same place. 

They keep the menu simple and do everything exceptional well. The pizzas are a healthy medium size with all the right ingredients and a thin floor and that flame oven taste. 

The burgers are as well on the small/medium side and really well put together. Fresh lettuce and juicy meat. What seems to be handmade sauces and well thought out combinations. 

And the fifth star is for the dynamite prawns - no idea why they are on the menu - but they are amazing. Not just some small shrimps tossed in some ready made sauce / no these are proper prawns cooked with talent and tossed in delicious sauce. 

Only for delivery and take away. Even the delivery speed was great every-time. I have and will recommend this place to everyone who loves a great pizza or a awesome burger.

review_text: [Therapeutic] Menagerie's most formidable talent lies not in the expertly(and exceptionally) prepared food, the tastefully decorated interiors or its applaudable soundtrack ( playing indie-hits like Lord Huron and Janis Ian) but rather in its ability to just 'be'. It doesn't try to propagate a faux sense of ostentatiousness or boast about its approach to healthy living. In this exclusive little mall called Mirdif 35, Menagerie is a place untouched by the too-easily damaged veneer of golden consumerism that plagues 99% of tinsel Dubai. As a regular customer, and a fan of everything from their raw doughnuts(vegan) to almond-milk lattes with agave and the refreshing acai bowl. Menagerie 35 exists to better your day, or at least that has been my personal experience. It is also worth mentioning that it is one of the only cafes where one can read a good book, unbothered.

review_text:
[Good for quick Indian food] I visited Bombay Chowpatty for a quick lunch recently. The quickness is the key aspect; you do get served quickly. Serving quantities too are quite good and sufficient to fill one up.

That said, one should stick to dosas or the like here as these are their best items. I found the gravies to be too oily and the same could be said about the pooris. It's a relatively no-frills place, but focusing on more healthy preparation would be a great help. The rava masala dosa we also had was quite good, no issues there.

review_text:
[Delicious Koobideh kababs] Hatam __ A lovely Iranian Restaurant in Dubai! They serve complimentary bread and green salad platter that consist of rocket leaves,mint leaves,walnuts, olives,feta cheese, and pickled veggies upon arrival.

I used cobone vouchers for my meal. This cobone deal is still on. You can save upto 50% on the bill by getting the vouchers. The menu card prices available on Zomato website for this restaurant need some updating as these are different from the prices mentioned on the Menu card at the restaurant.

I had the following dishes:

Lentil soup:  It was non spicy and tasted good.

Mixed salad: Though they serve basic salad platter complimentary but this 4 in 1 platter was really good. It had hummus, mutable, tabbouleh and seasonal salad in it.

Mixed Hatam salad: consisted of  dolma, humus topped with grilled meat, feta cheese and green mix salad. Loved it!

Arayes: Arayes is a middle eastern grilled pita bread stuffed with lamb mince, onions, tomatoes and spices. It was very delicious! But they forgot to serve fries with it as it was shown in the menu card’s Arayes picture.

Kabab Koobideh:  Special Iranian kababs served with rice were very juicy and tasty. They make the best koobideh kababs!!

Tikka and kabab:  Marinated lamb pieces served with rice were nicely grilled.

Falooda:  It was very chilled and refreshing!

Overall it was a nice experience. Seating facility is good. Walls are adorned with beautiful paintings. They also have family cabins for privacy. Our server was kind and friendly. He was happy to help us when we asked him for the extra plates and cutleries. I would love to come back here again.

#littlepakistanikitchen #instafoodie #dubaifoodies #arabiancenter

[Don't vacillate, just GO!!] Behara has hospitality experience in Switzerland and Dubai that I know of. He took over the extended family restaurant that has been around for 82 years a year ago when he bought out his uncles. He's converted it into a Lebanese/International locale with abundant charm! 

His mother makes the kebnehnayyeh fresh each morning and his dedication and passion are clearly evident. Everything we ate was fresh and served just right. If you're nice he may even bless you with a drop of his homemade vodka with cedar sap. Retsina like but so much better 🤩

Don't miss stopping here for any meal including breakfast and don't miss a walk through the park across the street to see the incredible cedar trees. It's worth the drive without a doubt. Bravo!!🙌

Deserts were good as well, especially the fresh waffles, chocolate ice cream and strawberries.

Thx Guys.

review_text:
[Tasty food and excellent service] We checked out this place following a recommendation by locals. It’s very close to the main touristy area of Byblos(Jbeil), but remains off the beaten path. If not walking there from the main souk and sites, the Uber ride is an affordable 4$! It’s along the main road to the Saint Charbel Monastery.

This is where the locals go for fresh, clean, affordable and tasty Lebanese food. We were two and split the fattouch (delicious), hummus, and the mixed grill. Everything is of top quality and the soujouk kebabs were particularly excellent. 

We would definitely come back and recommend Kabablaki as a place to check out and eat where locals eat.

We ate at the restaurant and it seemed to attract a lot of delivery orders (takeaway).  I  believe they have convenient hours since it stays open late.

review_text:
[ALWAYS SATISFYING EXCEPT WHEN............] Ksaar (for me and my family) is an annual must have dining every year we vacation in Lebanon.

The cuisine is plentiful with a wide variety of choices from Lebanese / Italian & generally Mediterranean options including grilled and seafood platters.

Kids menus are also available with a bonus playground onsite and monitored by restaurant staff, however, parental or nanny supervision is highly recommended for 
young children till the age of 7 in my opinion.

The local delicacies such as sujok and mhamraa, and kubbeh are fantastic with a glass of beer ;) Pizza's, fries and burgers are also available along with pasta  

Ksaar also has outdoor seating for those with a need of a hooka bubbly.

I recommend Ksaar and rate it highly.

review_text:
[Disappointed] Absolutely disappointing experience at El Molino.
Nothing tasted fresh — from the pork to the chicken, everything felt off. The pork lacked that juicy, flavorful taste you’d expect, and the chicken was just as underwhelming.

The tortilla bread had a moldy smell, which was honestly unacceptable. When we pointed it out, the staff took their time to respond and didn’t even bother to ask if the replacement was okay.

The service was careless and indifferent. No follow-up, no apology — it’s like they just didn’t care.
We left feeling disappointed and honestly a bit grossed out. Definitely not coming back.

review_text:
El Molino – A Slice of Mexico in the Heart of Byblos
Tucked in the charming old town of Byblos, El Molino is a refreshing and vibrant escape that brings the bold and colorful flavors of Mexico to the Lebanese coast. From the moment you step inside, you’re greeted by warm hospitality and a festive atmosphere that blends Mexican décor with a Mediterranean twist.
The menu is thoughtfully curated, offering a range of traditional Mexican dishes—from sizzling fajitas and loaded nachos, to authentic tacos al pastor, burritos, and enchiladas. Every dish bursts with flavor and is clearly crafted with fresh, quality ingredients. One standout is the guacamole, freshly made tableside and served with crispy homemade tortilla chips. Their margaritas—especially the spicy version—are not to be missed, offering the perfect balance of heat and citrus.

Service is friendly and attentive, with staff happy to explain dishes or offer recommendations. The ambiance is lively yet cozy, ideal for both casual outings and special celebrations. Whether you’re seated indoors or enjoying the outdoor terrace, the vibe remains inviting and relaxed.

I was really ashamed of having brought 4 foreigner friends to this place.

review_text: [undoubtedly the best fatoush of my life so far] We stumbled on this place due to their signs by the roadside. What luck and what a revelation. We had a set meal: the hummus was perfect, the babaganoush was to die for. Because we were wondering about it, we were shown how they smoked the aubergine. The fatoush was just incredible and contained such a variety of herbs leaves, some of which we didn't recognise. A perfect balance. Then long double cooked chips, calamari which was cleanly crisp outside and succulent tasty with no rubberiness. It was served with a variant of tartare sauce, pleasantly acidic. Then we had a variety of crisply fried smallish fish. Utterly delicious, too much for me. When we thought this was over, they brought us a delicious, crisp fried sweet pie, like a rose water flavoured Greek galactoburico. We were in heaven. it is all fresh, much of it home produce, the owners are fishermen. It cost less than half the price of restaurants on the harbour, yet this is just yards from the sea and so friendly.  One of those finds I would not tell you about if I lived more locally!

review_text:
[Lucky stop.]   We were walking from the Old Souk and looking for a place to eat that didn’t overlook the site of Ancient Byblos. Strolled by here and decided to drop in because it didn’t have an English name. It turned out to be a good choice.
  A very nice waiter came quickly over to the table with menus. There is quite a lot to choose from, but we decided on a salad for my wife and a mixed meat platter for us to share. Our food arrived quickly and we were pleased. It turned out to be a great stop. 
  We know food is a difficult subject to review. A restaurant is only as good as the last meal served, but this one pleased us. Nice staff, the food came quickly, and it tasted great.

review_text: [A gem seafood restaurant in Amchit] This is a very small restaurant that is part of a fishery on the coast of Amchit north of Byblos. The fishery is well known under the name of its owner Pierre Abboud but few people know that it is also a restaurant. The place is very small (8 tables only) and there is no decoration. Actually you can’t even see it from the road because it is hidden by a tree. But the food is so good. Of course, since Pierre is a fisherman you get the catch of the day. All fresh and perfectly cooked  and seasoned. We had a selection of calamari with garlic and parsley, fried fish and broiled sole. Accompanied by hummus and fattoush. It was all delicious. Service is fast. And the surprise: prices are way cheaper than the trove of fancy seafood restaurants dotting the Byblos-Amchit coast. Definitely a hidden gem.

review_text:
[Inedible sushi] As a Byblos resident living 5 min away from Mon Maki, I would caution against visiting this restaurant due to the alarming inconsistency in their food quality. The last time I ordered for pick-up, the sushi was horrible, and I'm not exaggerating when I say "horrible". Specifically, I ordered sushi rolls that included salmon, mango, and avocado. The mangos and avocados were not ripe at all. I'm talking apple textured mangos and rubber textured avocados. It's unbelievable that they could even have the audacity to serve them like that. Almost as if it was done on purpose. And to top it off, the rice was also undercooked, resulting in a horrible overall experience. We were 3 people and we couldn't eat more than two pieces.
I don't usually write reviews for restaurants, but with their ridiculous prices, I felt the responsibility to share my honest experience and warn other people. Especially after reaching out to the restaurant to express my concerns, and all they did was reply to me with "we'll call you back in a bit" (they never did btw).

review_text: [Hats off, from a usually fussy client!] The overall best place in Byblos if you like authentic, fresh and delicious Italian cuisine. This is the WOW place, I would say. Aesthetically a joy for the senses. The beautiful decor, the comfortable chairs, a cozy lighting and a busy open kitchen! You would commit a sin if during your stay in Byblos you don't have at least one meal here. I had a delicious freshly made Tiramisu here with an ice Americano. The Tiramisu is a one way ticket to heaven! Then I spotted a waiter grinding fresh pesto to go with freshly made bread, another work of magic. Highly attentive, courteous yet professional servers. It was delightful to see a number of tables celebrating reunions inside and on the outside patio of the restaurant. A kids' cubby house with a slide would keep parents happy and kids safe while healthy and fresh food is served. I wish I was staying longer in Byblos to sample their entire menu. Hats off, from a usually fussy client!

review_text:
[Broumana branch - disappointing] Duo has always been a great restaurant but their Broumana branch was a little disappointing. 
From a location perspective, it is actually very nicely located on the main road overlooking the heart of the action. The experience is really a nice experience to be on one of the tables at the border of the seating area.
The menu is mostly international with meals for every taste and a daily speciality. They had Escalope on the day I visited and it was not bad. 
The disappointing factor is mainly the prices. Everything is overpriced. A can of Pepsi is for LBP 5,000 (USD 3.5), which is a complete rip off when you actually can buy it for half a dollar
The food is nice but also quite overpriced for the quality and the taste. 
The service does not mark high neither. It was a little slow and took time for them to come and take out order. Also, I was surprised with the cleanliness of some of the utensils they provided us.

DUO brags on being a unique restaurant but my experience with the Broumana branch was quite disappointing. I recommend you stay away from this place until it gets better. You actually have a ton of other choices in the area at a walking distance.

review_text:
[Non Traditional Breakfast with Fresh Cunchy & Stuffed Meat Sandiwches, Friendly Atmosphere, Hidden Gems of Beirut,] Background:

It was recommended by one of my friends. His father used to take him there when he was a kid. We went together with other friends and we had our sandwiches feast. It is a ''non – traditional'' Lebanese way of having breakfast.
It is not a man2oushe nor hummus and foul. 
The breakfast here is sandwiches that range from the typical labne and cheese to meat sandwiches such as sujuk (sausages), roast beef, Sawda (the kidney of the chicken) and lsenet (roasted cow tongue).

What to Expect:

It is an old sandwich shop in Beirut:
-The place is small
-seating is basic: stool and aluminum bar
-decoration is minimal. 
-Suitable for a group of friends or a take away for the family.

My own Experience:

The sandwiches I tried were roast beef, sawda and sujuk. The three were crunchy and freshly baked but more importantly stuffed with meat and hence you enjoy the real taste of the sandwich.
I loved how he adds  fresh mint for all the sandiwches

Wait for the owner to call your number and stay aware of it as he will be asking you if you want it spicy or not? with mayonnaise mustard and pickles. The owner is friendly and has his own way in interacting with customers. 

The atmospheres is nice and friendly. Standing with other people waiting for your number and accordingly for the delicious sandwiches!!! A spontaneous chat with the one next to you is unavoidable.

Recommendations:

You have three sizes of sandwiches:
-Half a sandwich (recommended) (2$)
-A medium size sandwich 
-And a large sandwich (4$)
 
 I recommend you to have othe half sandwich option and order two different sandiwches. Afte tthe two i  can assure you you will be satisfyingly FULL!!!
If you want to take it further try the desserts sandwiches which are usually served at home such as jam and halawa

Timing: it is good to go there for breakfast or brunch. He closes in the early afternoon when all the food is sold out.

How to get there: Hidden in the narrow alleys of corniche Al Mazraa. Ask the taxi to drop you next to Paindore and when you reach paindore ask anyone and they will show you. (1 min walking distance)

review_text:
[(Liza) Beyrouth Je t'aime] I tasted arak for the first time at Liza’s ;)
Here I found myself seated in the intimate courtyard of a historic homestead, jasmine flowering in front of me, a fountain splashing behind me and then this thrilling taste of subdued sweetness of an anise drink on a balmy spring day (1st May).
Waiters in ‘Beyrouth je t’aime ’ tee shirts were graced with trays and platters bearing an array of dishes starting with a beautiful artichoke salad tossed with garden-fresh peas,coriander,spring onion and ending with a joyful rice and caramel pudding. In between many leisurely shared dishes with my friends,Lebanese-style hospitality,there was time to peek inside the alluring Liza mansion and buy one of those ‘je t’aime’ tee shirts to express my reaction to Beirut -- a city that took me by surprise. Aside from communicating my reviews,the effortless artistry and elegance at Liza’s and astounding authenticity of Lebanon at large,left me speechless.

review_text: [Fusion of elegance and authenticity] Liza in Beirut is a stunning fusion of elegance and authenticity, offering some of the finest Lebanese cuisine in the city. The standout dish of the evening was the beef manti—delicate dumplings packed with rich, savory flavors that left a lasting impression. Yet, beyond the food, it was Khalil who made the night truly exceptional. With his heartfelt warmth, genuine hospitality, and an instinctive care for every guest, he transformed the meal into an experience that felt less like dining out and more like being welcomed into a home. His thoughtful recommendations and effortless charm made every moment at Liza unforgettable.

review_text:
[Excellent Armenian / Lebanese Restaurant] If you want to try Lebanese Food with a slight twist, Almayass is a very nice option. The Armenian restaurant is nicely decorated, very cozy and the service is extremely attentive which I really liked. 
The dishes are quite similar to what you find in Lebanese restaurants but most of the recipes are cooked in a different way for example Kebab with sweet and sour cherry sauce. We ordered 4-5 different dishes and every single dish was really delicious. The price is ok, not overpriced but also not cheap. We paid roughly 60 USD for 2 person. 

The dessert was offered complimentary to us which was a nice detail. 

Thumbs up!

review_text:
[Great vegan restaurant] The restaurant is a bit hidden under an appartment block at the end of Hamra street, but well worth going. It is a very colourful place that is open 24/7, and serves vegan food exclusively. I’m an omnivore, but the taste of everything that was served was divine and at a great price.

They serve sandwiches, soups, mezze, pizza and pasta, and I was spoiled for choice. I took the lentil soup and the server recommended Intake the vegan cheese sticks with it. The sauces that were served with the cheese complimented them perfectly, especially the cashew-based sauce was finger-licking good. The soup was comforting and served with a fattoush salad. I also took one of the cheesecakes as dessert. Each dish cost around 140,000, which is very reasonable for the quality served

I highly recommend this place if you want some quality lebanese vegan food (or even if you’re an omnivore like me, because I didn’t miss anything on my plate).

review_text: [Phenomenal Crepes] This tiny shop provides the best crepes in Lebanon. I might sound a bit exaggerating but boy how satisfied I was after my visit to 101 Gourmet. For our Saturday night treat I headed to this place after hearing positive comments regarding the crepes they make. It's a tiny shop with only one crepe board machine. The employee was polite and helpful. He suggested I take their special pizza crepe and the white hershey's with lotus spread and digestive biscuit crashed chunks, I also took one additional crepe containing Nutella and halawa. For a start I noticed that the board on which the crepes are made is rectangular not round like the ones usually seen at crepes parlors. The employee does not spread the crepe liquid mix by hands but instead he puts it in a sort of container connected to the board and simply passes it above.  You won't find those crepes the gooey empty edges as they don't fold their crepes the usual way. The crepe dough isn't unpleasantly sweet and it's very well cooked having that lovely brown golden color. All the crepes we got were amazingly tasty. I noticed that for the Pizza crepe they use Barilla special pizza sauce and they add good quality Mozzarella cheese they add to that black olives, mushrooms and corn (which they make sure to remove the water from) and here you go !!! As for the sweet crepes they were marvelous especially the one recommended by the employee (white hershey's with lotus spread and digestive biscuit crashed chunks) !!! The crepes prices are cheaper here than the ones at the competitors. It's surely my new favorite Crepes spot in town.

review_text:
[Truly exceptional experience!] As soon as we stepped inside, the atmosphere was welcoming, and beautifully designed and set the tone to a memorable meal.
The perfect balance between cozy and elegant. The lighting, music, and decor all contributed to a relaxed, enjoyable environment that made us feel right at home.

The food was equally impressive. Every dish was fresh, flavorful, and perfectly cooked. You can really taste the care and quality in each bite. The quinoa and  shrimp was a standout, rich, and beautifully presented and the hummos with herbs was also very delicious.
In the other side, it was my first time tasting the fattet fawaregh, and I was honestly blown away. From the very first bite, the flavors were rich, perfectly balanced, and unexpectedly satisfying. The texture, the seasoning, the aroma, everything came together in the best way. I didn’t expect to love it this much, but it quickly became a new favorite. It was one of those meals you remember long after the last bite.

The staff were friendly and attentive without being intrusive. Overall, a fantastic dining experience. Highly recommended for anyone who appreciates great food in a great setting!

review_text:
[Reservation impossible for "only" 2 people? Seriously?] I am not judging the quality of the food, as I did not have the opportunity to eat there.

However, my experience with this restaurant was extremely disappointing. Their website claims to offer a high-end service, but the reservation form does not work. I tried contacting them via Instagram, email, and WhatsApp, but never received a response.

I then went to the restaurant in person to make a reservation, and a staff member refused to take my booking for two people, claiming there was a minimum of four guests, information that is nowhere indicated. Even worse, the person spoke down to me, suggesting that the $75 menu was probably “a bit expensive” for me.

Verdict: a huge lack of respect and an extremely disappointing service.

review_text:
[One of the last still standing nightclubs in Beirut] Nestled in one of the smallest streets in Ashrafyeh, Almodovar quickly became one of the busiest pub/night clubs in Beirut. And there’s a reason for this. 
To start with the food is quite interesting with modern fusion Mexican menus and is better than one would expect in a night club. Literally everything is presented with exotic dips that will make your taste buds dance throughout the meal. Also, they have one of the best drinks menus for such a venue. You can order almost any kind of drink or cocktail and you will surely get it. 
However this NOT what makes Almodovar really interesting. The secret is at post 10pm this very particular place transforms into a dancing floor where one can dance to one hearts delight. The music selection is exquisite and on point and the crowds will definitely go wild. THIS is Almodovar’s little secret and it’s a good one. 
Still, a few little details can make or break the mood. For one, some high tables are too small and uncomfortable especially if it’s a group of 3+ pax. Also, on some special nights it is truly overcrowded. Claustrophobic people should avoid. 
Still it is a must do experience. Word of the wise make sure you reserve a good table. My personal recommendation is to try it on a Thursday or Sunday. Both are exceptional nights there. Enjoy!

[Good Service and Food] We ended up in this restaurant without aiming at it really. I am not sure it has an indoor seating as most service and people were outdoor, on a side walk. Nicely designed so you do not really feel the "side Walk" effect.
Service was great, very professional with a lot of attention to the patrons. Very friendly too.
Prices are on a high however and we opted for cocktails rather than wine because of the prices. Same bottle the night before somewhere else at $45.00 was at $75.00!!!
Still cocktails were not cheap, but this is the way in Beirut for any restaurant that wants itself fancy and trendy.
I had a fish that was nicely and perfectly cooked, very fresh. My wife had a salad and she liked it too.
Recommendable but a bit expensive

They tried to improve our experience and compensated us with complimentary makis. They even had a chef to come and roll fresh makis for us, which were delicious and tasted very fresh. Thanks to how they handled the situation, our experience ended very positive. If they keep the same quality and service as at the end of our visit, this is definitely a place I would recommend to try the AYCE conveyor belt. The staff is very welcoming, helpful, and truly caring.

review_text:
[Capitale Café: One of Beirut’s most elegant café experiences] Capitale Café is honestly my favorite place and I always find myself going back. The atmosphere is warm, stylish, and perfect whether you’re meeting friends or just enjoying some quiet time. The food is consistently delicious; from their savory items to their desserts, everything tastes fresh and well prepared. What I love the most is the presentation; every plate looks beautiful and carefully done, which makes the whole experience even better.
The team is always helpful, welcoming, and attentive without being overwhelming. You really feel taken care of. During Ramadan, they also offer a special Iftar formula for those who love to break their fast in a cozy yet elegant setting, with a great selection and generous portions.
For me, Capitale isn’t just a café; it’s a place I keep coming back to. Highly recommended.

review_text:
[Disappointing service and poor handling of customer concerns. Management should act.] Our experience at this restaurant in Dbayeh was disappointing due to multiple service-related issues.

Upon arrival, the valet was sitting in a parked car and did not assist with parking. After waiting approximately 10 minutes, we parked our car on the road ourselves. Despite this, the valet later approached us requesting parking fees without providing a parking ticket!

Later in the evening, the valet came to our table inside the restaurant and began arguing with us about the parking fee in an unprofessional and impolite manner. Restaurant staff did not intervene. Although we paid the requested amount, no parking ticket was issued! 

When settling the restaurant bill, we paid using $100 bills and expected to receive change. The waiter did not return the change! We waited and waited but he did not bring the change . Only after calling him back did he bring the change, without any apology. Tipping should always be at the customer’s discretion and should not be assumed.

We positively raised these concerns with the manager, including:
	•	The valet parking issue
	•	Food quality and presentation
	•	The handling of the bill and change

While the manager listened, no corrective action or resolution was offered.

We hope management reviews these issues seriously and takes steps to improve service standards and customer experience.

review_text:
[Interrogation] If I was to rate the welcome and service, I would put ZERO. But to be fair for the Dj and the lovely music I will put half the grade.

What a shame, I called to inquire about reservation, the guy told me to be as early as 9:30 to guarantee spot. When we arrived, the bouncers at the main entrance of building started interrogating us (three ladies) as if we are at the prison entrance! Stuff like who are you here for?! and why are you here that early, until he called them to check if they are ready to receive?! And so we were granted the permission to cross the borders.
We arrived at the 6th floor where the staff was hardly ready. No welcome whatsoever, we sat at the bar and the waitress as well as another waiter who both lack training, approached us. They said that they don't have drinks menu.
We ordered:
- One  Moscow mule that came with an average taste and no cucumber (not available)
-Another friend ordered rosé yet the waiter poured her white wine; she had to ask for him to replace it
- Seven up diet that was initially  served in a plastic cup until i asked her why not the normal glass one?!
They have a policy where you are requested either to pay each drink straight or to deposit the visa card until departure. Maybe they had few bad experiences. But the way they ask for it is not really appropriate; it was as if looking at the customer in his eye doubting him and unconsciously accusing him of theft!
 
The enjoyable thing is the Music, the age range is mid -late twenties.

I think that they have a potential with a beautiful location, they need an adequate training of the staff, a less suspicious attitude but most importantly more respectful bouncers.
P.S:  Don't drag people early if you are not ready to receive.

I wholeheartedly recommend it for anyone looking to enjoy a wonderful time in the company of kind and truly exceptional people.

review_text: [It's not Just Better It's the Best] Finally a bakery in Lebanon that produces excellent/super tasty cookies, cinnamon rolls, brownies and many other delicious treats exactly the real American way like they should be done. Bravo. I have almost lost hope in getting decent cookies after tasting several bakeries productions in Beirut. Until I read great reviews about this place. I went there with no high expectations but luckily I was amazed. It's a small cute little shop in a clam area in Beirut. The goodies are very well displayed. The lady who helped me was friendly and informative. I grabbed one cinnamon roll, one kinder filled cookie, one caramel (Twix) brownie and finally one cake ball filled with Nutella. I can safely say that all what I have purchased were amazingly tasty. It's clear that this bakery uses only premium quality ingredients and it has the real American know how. The cookie is big, chewy and generously filled. The cake ball filled with Nutella and dipped in chocolate is out of this world. The brownie was rich and moist. Cinnamon roll was fresh and full of flavors. Portions are more than fair and the prices for those jewels are so reasonable. I so recommend this bakery.

I can safely say that all what I have purchased were amazingly tasty. It's clear that this bakery uses only premium quality ingredients and it has the real American know how. The cookie is big, chewy and generously filled. The cake ball filled with Nutella and dipped in chocolate is out of this world. The brownie was rich and moist. Cinnamon roll was fresh and full of flavors. Portions are more than fair and the prices for those jewels are so reasonable. I so recommend this bakery.

review_text:
[A beautiful way to spend one’s last evening in Beirut] We decided to have Italian food for our last dinner in Beirut. We chose this place as its near to our hotel.
The restaurant is small. Just five tables on the ground floor . Upstairs, i think there is a kitchen, no idea about seating.
The host Samir is genuinely friendly and passionate about the food. I could not decide so he gave me a few options. Anyway, we chose veg gnocchi, a spaghetti bolognese and a meat lasagna. Absolutely divine and when teamed up with white Heritage sweet wine, it was an unbeatable combination.
Food was relatively expensive but outstanding. Well worth the cost. 
As we asked for a cab and said it was our last night, Samir whipped off the table cloth and said that foreigners who visit his place are requested to follow the tradition! He made us write a comment in our language on the wooden table top. 

A great way to leave Beirut with wonderful memories. Thank you, Samir.

review_text:
[... and you want to have a very enjoyable dinner or breakfast I strongly suggest that you try The Olive Garden...] Within the Rotana Gefinor Hotel in Beirut is a fantastic restaurant called The Olive Garden.
We had a fantastic dinner and great breakfast there last week during our short stay at the Rotana Gefinor.
For dinner, the F&B Manager had arranged a special table that had rose petals on the table and had added a few small candles to have a very romantic effect. 
My wife ordered a plate of barbecued shrimp, vegetables, and a large basket of French Fries. She said it was the best shrimp dinner that she had ever tasted!
I ordered their famous Caesar Salad and a bowl of soup. The salad was so large that I had to cancel the soup, as I was too full on this great tasting Ceasar Salad. 
I enjoyed several glasses of the Olive Gardens house white wine, from the Lebanese vineyard called: St. Thomas. 
The next morning, we enjoyed a very good breakfast.
We spent one night at the Gefinor Rotana hotel from our return from our Egypt Holiday, and again the food within the Olive Garden was fantastic.
So, if you find yourself in Beirut and you want to have a very enjoyable dinner or breakfast I strongly suggest that you try The Olive Garden restaurant with in the Rotana Gefinor Hotel, trust me you will not be sorry, trust me.

 The Olive Garden serves a Breakfast and Luncheon Buffet plus an A la Carte Dinner menu.
I  enjoyed their  breakfast buffet during my last stay and all the food was prepared in a very professional manner. My Omelet was made to order, and it was very tasty.

So, if you want to enjoy a very nice buffet Breakfast I suggest that you try the Olive Garden restaurant and terrace.
Trust me you will enjoy the Olive Garden restaurant.

review_text:
[... that you have both meals within the hotel at the fantastic Olive Garden restaurant, you will not be sorry, trust me.] The Olive Garden is the main restaurant within the Rotana Gefinor Hotel in the Hamra district of Beirut.
The Olive Garden serves a wonderful Buffet Breakfast, and during our short stay both their lunch and dinner meals were using their Ala Carte Menu only as I assume the hotels occupancy rate was a bit low. I assume that once the occupancy rate increases they will go back to having all three meals in the Buffet style.
The Olive Garden has a lovely outdoor terrace with a nice water fountain and many flowering plants. After our two hour walk outside the hotel, my wife and I returned back to the hotel and decided to have our dinner on the outside terrace. The Ala Carte menu was simply perfect as a full buffet would simply be too much food for each of us. 
My wife ordered a very nice grilled Chicken, with a side of vegetables and a hugh amount of very tasty French Fries. I started with a nice vegetarian Vegetable Soup and then a very delicious Margaretta Pizza with fresh mushrooms. The size of the all portions were so large both my wife and I could not finish our entrée’s. 
The next morning I enjoyed the Rotana’s special Breakfast Buffet. I started with a nice plate of sliced fresh fruits followed by a great mixed vegetable Omelet. 
All-in-all it was a fantastic way to start my day as I had three important meetings planned for that day and I never developed any hunger pains throughout the day.
I strongly suggest that instead of going out of the hotel and having your breakfast and dinner at some unknown restaurant that you have both meals within the hotel at the fantastic Olive Garden restaurant, you will not be sorry, trust me.

review_text:
[Come as a group of at least four. Order a little of everything.] Beirut is noticeably on the mend after the 2020 port explosion that devasted the city leaving hundred’s dead. With a favourable terroir, land and sea, and some decades of French influence, you can look forward to great food.

There’s a simplicity in great Japanese restaurants that adheres to the adage, same, same, but different. The décor is very simple and no less elegant and contemporary for it. Located between Zaitunay Bay, Downtown and the political district, it services a demanding clientele.

That the menu has no prices on the printed version might make a first-time visitor uneasy, but worry not. Due to currency instability, which has led to the beginning of nationwide dollarisation, pricing can be fluid in Lebanese Lira. 

There is a QR code that will give you current menu pricing in US Dollars. This is very common and should not worry you. You will be far too interested in the fine range of offerings, the wines every bit as appealing.

Sea salted edamame for the immediately famished enjoyed, it was time to place both feet firmly inside.

Fresh salmon maki put a smile on my wife’s face, pre and post, much like a child getting a small ice cream cone, albeit this one in wrapped glorious dark seaweed.

Ordered from the salad menu, odd given the greens were really tasty garnish, were two helpings of crispy spicy salmon. Sashimi salmon in popcorn popping panko crumb and a light late lingering spice. If we were ordering in stages, I’m not sure we’d have eaten anything else.

Tuna and salmon sashimi were on point, fresh ginger and wasabi likewise.

Three separate sushi platters arrived next. There were a few of us. I’m not sure I’ve seen a mango ura maki before. The colour was joyful and like its neighbours disappeared systemically into us happy diners.

I opted for the wagyu foie gras, ordered and served bleu. Merci chef. Both were similarly butchered for their plating, served as two fractured asteroids one atop the other. Holding it together was a thuddingly thickened teriyaki sauce. From the nose up to my eyes, everything was alight. 

It cut like butter and melted similarly in all our mouths. As I write this an hour later, its ghost still happily haunts my mouth.

The service is good, efficient, the waiters know their menu and can talk for it.

I don’t know when I’ll be back in Beirut again, but if I am, I’ll dine here at least once.

review_text:
[Wow!!] Beirut is a little tricky as far as location is concerned. This restaurant is somehwat hidden away, but don't let that put you off. Is is easy to locate on google maps or on Allo taxi. It is small, boutique and very, very friendly. 

So much for location.

But here is the kicker. The staff are attentive without being overbearing. Arabic, French and English are spoken with equivalent fluency. There are brilliant starters and coffee and desserts are available. But the real deal here are the steaks. 

Yes; you will pay for what you get; It is not cheap, but when it comes it is sublime. Or better. I will confess I had an Argentinian 200g fillet and chips. It was quite simply wonderful. 

I was in Beirut for two weeks and would (and will) go back here to get a real, quality and carefully well-cooked steak which you could compare with anything you could get in London, Munich, Sydney or Cape Town.

LBP, USD and mainstream credit cards are welcome (with a + for international credit cards)

If  you are hankering for a clearly wonderful beef meat meal with 100% quality, then this is your place.

review_text:
[Gluten Free Deli.co Delight] My brother, his wife, kids & I went to Deli.co early on in December for breakfast in downtown Beirut in a Saturday.

The place is average sized - although the outdoor seating arrangement felt a little crowded and in close proximity to other patrons. 

The waiter was pretty helpful and offered us plenty of advice and a quick service. 

I ordered gluten free waffles & was truly truly impressed at how yummy they are, while me niece ordered scrambled eggs, which were pretty moist. My sister in law loved her avo on toast while my brother found his pancakes mediocre. I also ordered a gluten free pb&j with caramelized banana which was alright. 
The the star dish, and a must try of you try this place, was the gluten free waffles; everyone agreed that they were the best waffles we’ve tasted in a longtime - not to mention gluten free!  

Over all, Deli.co’s outdoor seating arrangement had a chill vibe, especially for a Saturday and the food was tasty.

review_text:
[Delightful] Deli.co is one of the four sisters restaurants Pizza.co, Burger Co and recently opened Ummi at Beirut souks. And it is not to be underestimated!

Same concept of ability to order from all four menus, we opted to honor Deli.co's menu only, and we did not regret!

Service is synchronized with the theme, the staff are knowledgeable off the menu, cheerful and helpful.
We enjoyed dinner on the terrace, savoring:

Signature item: the original NYC Pastrami on rye bread served with a side of dill pickles and chips: it is so flavorsome not the healthiest thing you can have so worth the try!
Open faced scottish Smoked salmon over spicy avocado on country bread, equally served with chips:premium quality of every single ingredient reflected in the taste
Classic grilled cheese: same presentation and sides
My favorite trio salad: first of all I loved the concept of being able to combine three types of salad. I also appreciated the generous quantity and in addition to that the fact that it is changed daily: we had a combination of grilled vegetables, freekeh salad with eggplants, pomegranate, cherry tomatoes and parsley and fell in love at first sight / bite! and lettuce hearts with olives and drizzled with yogurt based sauce. 
How could we miss their home made desserts:

Lemon poppy seed cake: I love the contrast sourness of lemon with the sweetness of cake and the small crunch of the seeds
Semolina cake with caramelized oranges: so moist.
Carrot cake: was my least favorite I felt it needed more spices though it had a beautiful presentation.
We had lovely times and would totally recommend you to visit.

La Petite

XoXo

review_text:
[Disappointed] Well, this was disappointing. Went with two Lebanese people. After 15 plus years, we are always keen to find a really good Lebanese / Middle Eastern restaurant. This is not it.

The menu is straight forward - all the basics one would expect: Hummus, baba ghanoush, sambousik, kibbeh, shish taouk, and so on.  We were very excited to try the kibbeh nayeh (lamb tartare) which they said they didn’t have, because the quality of the meat wasn’t good enough. I can tell them where to get good enough lamb in Singapore, if they are struggling. The menu is pretty short anyway, so not to offer everything on it on a Saturday evening seems an odd choice.

The food generally is mediocre. Sadly. The fattoush had an odd aftertaste - like some of the lettuce wasn’t as fresh as it should have been. The garlic sauce with the chicken skewers didn’t taste the way it should. Perhaps there are different ways to prepare, but it ought to have a lemony taste. This one didn’t. We usually love it, always eat too much of it. This thou tasted strange and bland. Everything was a bit bland - we can make much better and fresher Lebanese food at home.

We had two cocktails which were supposed to have alcohol, but we could not really taste it. It tasted mostly of water. Two bears came and were so flat, we sent them back - we rarely sent anything back. Should have done the same with the cocktails. We got the safer option of two Little Creatures bottles instead.

The interior of the place is pretty, very small place though. We had hoped to sit outside as it looked pleasant in photos, ans we generally prefer outside seating. Unfortunately, that area is right next to the car drop off point, and not sufficiently protected, so its really just a waiting area. Service was good though. They have enough staff and they are friendly and effective.

I don’t think we will be back though. Why bother if they are not going to make an effort with this to being this amazing cuisine to Singapore.

review_text:
[A splash of color on the table!] We tried Spicy No 7 just before the COVID lockdown. We had ordered home a variety of dishes according to the suggestions of the manager. What a fragrant surprise! 
I have to say beforehand that the delivery service was prompt and neat. Dishes arrived hot and not spilled despite the sauces, which in my dictionary is a major plus. 
The food is colorful, tasty and quite interesting. The curry is well balanced the spices are fragrant and fresh. The rice is made to perfection. The only drawback was actually the samosas that were in my opinion at bit over fried. This been said, my opinion is favorable to this small gem as the country needs some “exotic” menus since the market is already saturated with French european cuisines and the Lebanese ones. 
Word of the wise, put all the clichés you heard about Indian cuisine and try it!

review_text:
[What a great place! Reminds] What a great place! Reminds me why the Lebanese street food culture is just great, and it’s the people not the money thrown at it that makes an eatery great! 

We sat outside with the wife and the 3 year old, and I just love sitting on a beirut sidewalk and watch life happen. We ordered the Samosas for appetizer and they were exactly how they should be and not as heavy as others that we’ve had.
The boy loves his chicken nuggets, but doesn’t really know what a chicken nugget is. So any breaded chicken breast does the trick. And he devoured the chicken escalope that we ordered for him. The wife and I shared the 7 burger, coconut curry and the delicious fries. They were all great but the burger is something of a must try, the typical “lebanese all in burger” wrapped in the Paratha is a brilliant idea and works like magic. This what happens when you thoughtfully design a dish and create a menu, a great hidden gem that lets the food shine, and does away with the need of a fancy or extravagant experience that inevitably takes away from the meal

review_text: [Best value for your money!] I loved my experience savoring authentic food at this tiny neighborhood restaurant. The welcome was warm and the decor is bright, clean and cheery. The food was delivered quickly with a smile, and I loved the clay plates it was served on. I tried their butter chicken, samosas, coconut chicken and they were all excellent; I did not feel stuffed afterwards. The chef had eliminated most oil/butter from his kitchen and replaced it with yogurt, coconut milk or cashew butter. The food is more flavorful then spicy (as in hot), which suited me just fine!  The restaurant is located in Gemmayze, in an airy corner on Pasteur st.

review_text:
[Extremely Discriminatory & Humiliating Experience] I had a reservation under my name, and my friends and I arrived properly dressed and ready for a night out. Yet, we were shocked by the extremely unprofessional and discriminatory treatment at the door.

I was denied entry because my leggings had a small tear on the side — something I immediately addressed by wearing a long jacket and clearly stating that I would not remove it inside. Even after that, they still refused to let me in.
Then they began judging my friends’ outfits as “over,” even though they were fully decent and properly dressed. Meanwhile, we watched other guests enter with outfits far more revealing — to the point where three-quarters of their bodies were exposed. There are pictures that clearly show the double standards.

This behavior is not only unprofessional — it is humiliating.
It shows a serious lack of consistency, respect, and integrity in how they choose who gets in.

The floor manager, Toufik , made the situation even worse. He gave disrespectful looks toward my friends and justified letting in a girl wearing a dress shorter than her sensitive areas by saying she was “properly dressed,” while claiming that leggings — clean, neat, and styled for a night out — “don’t fit the place.”
He even commented that leggings are only for the gym or cleaning floors. This is beyond disrespectful.

This is clear discrimination and humiliation, and it reflects an attempt to “protect the venue’s image” at the expense of people who are actually decent and well-presented — while allowing in outfits that are far more inappropriate.

Instead of respecting their guests, they choose to belittle them, misjudge them, and apply rules selectively depending on who is standing at the door. A place that treats customers like this should rethink what “reputation” truly means, because respect doesn’t come from insulting decent people.

This experience was unacceptable on every level.
No one should be spoken to or treated this way — especially with a confirmed reservation. If this is their idea of hospitality, they would be better off closing their doors and rethinking their values.

Joanne Wright

review_text:
[Seafood in Tyre] An excellent local restaurant, chefs do an excellent job with all of the many varieties of fish they offer.  Some say anyone can cook fish, yes, but these guys are really careful and consistent, so it is not over or undercooked.  Salads are also very good, and the grilled eggplant is amongst the best anywhere.  Chicken livers are also very well prepared.   
My most regular meal is a mezze, tabbouleh, rocca, grilled eggplant, chicken livers, and then some malefeh (small grilled barracuda), or sultan ibrahim fish.  
There is also a full range of meat dishes, and I had good lamb on skewers with a friend several times.

review_text:
[Terrible experience at Saida Rest House] Terrible food, terrible service!
I went with my family on the 8th of June 2025, at 9:00 PM, during Eid al-Adha. I understand that the staff might be tired throughout the day and especially in eid but it’s never an excuse to treat customers that way that we were treated!

First, the staff had grim and very grumpy faces, which was super unprofessional! We didn’t say anything, we ordered food and it came quickly ( not surprised since they had barely anyone there except us probably due to their terrible service).

We ordered variety of food from grilled chicken to hot meza, and everything was terrible, anyone that intends to go to such place would expect amazing food and service but both were non existent. We didn’t give them any notes to their food or service we just sat and enjoyed the view.

It all went down to when they placed the check, without us asking for it, but it was fine, it was 11:40 i think. We started dividing the total based on what everyone ordered. They kept coming every 2 minutes asking if the check was ready and we told them that we still need some time until it became 11:50 and the cashier that closes the system came and started shouting at us! At first we were shocked, earlier we asked one of the staff what time do they close he said that when all customers leave, so he didn’t have any right to come barging on us with his terrible attitude demanding to pay the check so he could close the system and go home! That’s your job and like it or not you have to wait, especially for such restaurant. Plus the restaurant says that they close at 12:00 Am so we weren’t passed that time!

And the manager in charge or the one responsible for the reception came and told us that the waiter that told us that they close whenever all costumers leave doesn’t know, which was illogical how would we know that he doesn’t know! His response represents the restaurant so if you wanna blame someone blame your staff and yourself for the ingenuity and unprofessionalism!

We paid and went out, on our way out other customers were leaving too and they also said “m3ash yshofo wejna” which means that they won’t see our faces again probably they had a terrible experience too!

So for anyone that thinks of going there, don’t, it’s not worth the money spent and you could spend way less amount for better food and better service elsewhere. Never again!!

First, the staff had grim and very grumpy faces, which was super unprofessional! We didn’t say anything, we ordered food and it came quickly ( not surprised since they had barely anyone there except us probably due to their terrible service). We ordered variety of food from grilled chicken to hot meza, and everything was terrible, anyone that intends to go to such place would expect amazing food and service but both were non existent. We didn’t give them any notes to their food or service we just sat and enjoyed the view. It all went down to when they placed the check, without us asking for it, but it was fine, it was 11:40 i think. We started dividing the total based on what everyone ordered. They kept coming every 2 minutes asking if the check was ready and we told them that we still need some time until it became 11:50 and the cashier that closes the system came and started shouting at us! At first we were shocked, earlier we asked one of the staff what time do they close he said that when all customers leave, so he didn’t have any right to come barging on us with his terrible attitude demanding to pay the check so he could close the system and go home! That’s your job and like it or not you have to wait, especially for such restaurant. Plus the restaurant says that they close at 12:00 Am so we weren’t passed that time! And the manager in charge or the one responsible for the reception came and told us that the waiter that told us that they close whenever all costumers leave doesn’t know, which was illogical how would we know that he doesn’t know! His response represents the restaurant so if you wanna blame someone blame your staff and yourself for the ingenuity and unprofessionalism! We paid and went out, on our way out other customers were leaving too and they also said “m3ash yshofo wejna” which means that they won’t see our faces again probably they had a terrible experience too! So for anyone that thinks of going there, don’t, it’s not worth the money spent and you could spend way less amount for better food and better service elsewhere. Never again!!
area: Old Saidon Souk Next To The Sea Castle

review_text:
[Zawat review ask alot before you order] Very disappointed with our recent visit to Zawat. As frequent visitors to Lebanon and long-time patrons of this restaurant, we were shocked to be charged nearly $250 just for fish. The menu listed prices without specifying they were per kilo, the fish wasn’t weighed in front of us, and no price was confirmed before preparing the order. Locals in Saida confirmed we were overcharged — something that feels like we were taken advantage of as foreigners.

To make matters worse, we were charged for parking despite using the restaurant’s own facilities. We’ve dined at many other well-known restaurants across Lebanon, with larger groups and similar meals, and never paid anywhere near this amount.

It’s not about the money — it’s about feeling respected and treated fairly. Sadly, this experience left us feeling scammed and extremely disappointed.

review_text:
[Chilled and relaxing] Located on a nice sandy beach with the clear sea. People are very relaxed and nice. 
Plenty of parking space. 
We sat at the far end of the beach bar, closer to do sea.
Otherwise, live DJ music is loud if you want to chat with the friends. Not that it’s a bad thing as it brings atmosphere up but not the best choice if you want to chat.
The waiters are nice and friendly. The sea food was fresh and decently prepared.
The drinks, especially the ones with alcohol, are inconsistent. Every new order of gin&tonic had less and less gin in it.
It’s not that it bothered us much as we truly enjoyed the location and experience overall so visited two days in a row.
Absolutely amazing sunset!!!!

review_text:
[Fresh juices, delicious ice cream, good prices and a local atmoshpere] You wanna feel the vibes of Tyre and its locals??
You wanna watch the typical Lebanese family outings: kids playing and parents enjoying themselves?
Niser Juice & Ice-cream have it all. The taste and the atmosphere combined with good prices. 
Did you have an enjoyable day in Tyre? Bathed in The sea and kissed sun? or strolled the ottoman souk and wandered in the colorful old Christian quarter? 
However your trip cannot end without refreshing yourself and boosting a bit your energy or your calories intake by visiting the Niser Juice & Ice-cream. Friendly staff, fresh yummy juice (especially the avocado) and  delicious Arabic/ Italian ice-cream. Whatever you choose Niser Juice will not disappoint you. 
Niser Juice & Ice-cream is the happy ending of a day trip to tyre. Order your juice then go sit at the nearby sea to and watch the sunset or relax in the air-conditioned hall of the Niser.

[Terrible service. Food not at all 5 stars] Probably the worst 5 star restaurant I have ever visited.
The waiters were totally unfamiliar with the menu. Couldn't explain basic things we asked.
We were 4 . 3 drinks arrived OK. The 4th never arrived untill we asked for it again
